news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate has dropped to 4.6%, marking a significant improvement from the previous month's 5.2%. The economy added a total of 250,000 jobs in various sectors, with the most notable increases seen in the tech industry, retail, and hospitality. Additionally, wage growth has remained steady at 3.2% year-over-year. The positive trend in employment numbers is being attributed to increased consumer spending and business investments. Overall, the job market continues to show resilience and growth, providing opportunities for both job seekers and employers.

The firm, known for its focus on enterprise, fintech, healthcare, and climate sectors, is aiming to leverage its global expertise to boost innovation in the region. Furthermore, B Capital will collaborate with the Qatar Investment Authority (“QIA”), the sovereign wealth fund of the State of Qatar, to support and enhance these initiatives.

news.headline: Merz promises fast government formation after projections indicate his victory. #elections #government
news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that approximately 15% of adults in the country are currently unemployed, with the highest rates seen among individuals aged 18-29. The study also found that 25% of households are experiencing food insecurity, highlighting the significant economic challenges facing many families. Furthermore, the research indicated that over 40% of small businesses have permanently closed due to the ongoing financial strains caused by the pandemic. These findings underscore the urgent need for government intervention and support to address the escalating unemployment and financial instability affecting individuals and businesses across the country.

news.headline: $1.4B Ethereum stolen in largest crypto hack ever; 401,347ETH lost. SEC establishes Cyber & Emerging Tech Unit.
news.summary: A staggering $1.4 billion worth of Ethereum was stolen in what is being described as the largest cryptocurrency hack in history. Bybit, a cryptocurrency exchange, fell victim to a major breach resulting in the theft of 401,347 ETH ($1.12 billion), along with 90,376 stETH ($253 million), 15,000 cmETH ($44 million), and 8,000 mETH ($23 million) from their Ethereum cold wallet. This incident surpasses the 2021 Poly Network hack which involved $611 million. In response, Bybit has launched a recovery bounty program offering up to 10% of the recovered funds to ethical cyber and network experts.

news.headline: AS Ekspress Grupp's Q4 2024 revenue rose by 10% to EUR 23.5 million, EBITDA increased by 23% to EUR 5.2 million. For the full year, revenue increased by 4% to EUR 76.2 million, with EBITDA up by 4% to EUR 10.7 million. Digital revenue increased by 9%, comprising 86% of total revenue in 2024, surpassing the 85% target set for 2026.
news.summary: AS Ekspress Grupp reported a 10% year-over-year increase in revenue for the 4th quarter of 2024, totaling EUR 23.5 million, while EBITDA also saw a significant rise of 23% to EUR 5.2 million. Over the whole year of 2024, revenue increased by 4% to EUR 76.2 million and EBITDA saw a similar growth of 4% reaching EUR 10.7 million. Notably, digital revenue experienced a 9% increase, constituting 86% of the Group’s total revenue for 2024, surpassing the target of 85% set for digital revenue by 2026.

news.headline: CESUR Mining expands cloud mining services for users worldwide, serving 9M+ members & contributing 3.8% of Bitcoin’s computing power.
news.summary: CESUR Mining, a cloud mining company, is expanding its services to make cryptocurrency mining easier and more profitable for users worldwide. Since its establishment in 2018, CESUR Mining has grown to serve over 9 million members globally, contributing about 3.8% of Bitcoin's total computing power. The platform operates with over 100 mining farms and 500,000 high-performance mining machines to ensure security, reliability, and sustainability. CESUR Mining utilizes clean energy sources like solar and wind energy to power its operations, reducing electricity costs for users. Users can participate in remote cryptocurrency mining by purchasing CESUR miner contracts, offering a more efficient alternative to traditional mining methods. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: 'Gary Cardone' sells 30,000 XRP at $2.71, exits position, doubts XRP army will get rich, shares investing principles.
news.summary: Renowned pseudonymous investment influencer 'Gary Cardone,' distinct from Grant Cardone, revealed his exit from the XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) market. He sold off 30,000 XRP units at $2.71 per unit on Friday, following six guiding principles formed throughout his 40-year finance career. In a viral post, Cardone expressed his well wishes for the XRP community to achieve great wealth despite his skepticism, stating, “I genuinely hope the XRP army gets stupid rich, I don’t think you will, but my best hopium is out to each of you.” For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: SEC Agrees to Drop Enforcement Case against Coinbase (NASDAQ: COIN), CEO Brian Armstrong claims it was unjustified.
news.summary: Coinbase Inc. CEO Brian Armstrong recently revealed that the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has decided to completely drop its enforcement case against the company. The lawsuit, which accused Coinbase of operating as an unregistered securities exchange, broker, and clearing agency, as well as violating securities laws with its staking-as-a-service program, was described by Armstrong as baseless and an unfair attack on the cryptocurrency industry. In an interview with CNBC, Armstrong emphasized that standing up for their customers' rights was crucial to prevent potential damage to the crypto industry in the United States. This development marks a significant win for Coinbase and the 50 million Americans who hold cryptocurrencies, as per Armstrong.

news.headline: Arbor Realty Trust announces Q4 and full year 2024 results, declares $0.43/share dividend.
Bybit founder confirms $1.4 billion Ethereum hack, 401,347 ETH ($1.12 billion) lost, claims masked transaction responsible.
news.summary: Ben Zhou, the founder of Bybit, has confirmed a significant security breach at the cryptocurrency exchange, resulting in a staggering $1.4 billion hack involving Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH). This breach stands out as one of the largest in recent times. The attack, aimed at Bybit's Ethereum cold wallet, led to the loss of 401,347 ETH ($1.12 billion), 90,376 stETH ($253 million), 15,000 cmETH ($44 million), and 8,000 mETH ($23 million) as reported by Onchain Lens. The breach occurred due to a manipulation of Bybit's multisig wallet interface, where signers were deceived into approving a fraudulent smart contract update without their knowledge. Ben Zhou explained that the transaction was disguised, showing the correct address on the user interface with the URL from Safe, but the signing message actually initiated a change in the smart contract logic of their ETH cold wallet. This inadvertent authorization by Bybit's team allowed the attackers to fully control the cold wallet and execute transfers. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

The Senate HELP Committee will scrutinize her qualifications and suitability for the role she is being considered for. This is a crucial juncture for Chavez-DeRemer as she looks to secure the position that will allow her to contribute her expertise and experience in service to the public. The outcomes of this hearing could have far-reaching implications, impacting not only Chavez-DeRemer's future but also the area of work she is being appointed to. This hearing is a pivotal moment in her career and will be closely watched by many.

news.headline: Global RF Gallium Nitride market projected to reach $2.8B by 2028, with a CAGR of 12.9%. Rise attributed to technology advancements and increasing adoption in military, defense, and aerospace sectors. High material costs are a challenge.
news.summary: The global RF gallium nitride market is witnessing significant growth, with a value of USD 1.3 billion in 2022 expected to reach USD 2.8 billion by 2028, showing a CAGR of 12.9%. This growth is driven by advancements in GaN technologies, its suitability for RF applications, and increased adoption in military, defense, and aerospace sectors. Important industry players include Sumitomo Electric Device Innovations, Qorvo, WOLFSPEED, NXP Semiconductors, and MACOM. One key trend is the expanding use of GaN in renewable energy applications, particularly solar power systems, for improved power densities. However, challenges persist, such as high material and fabrication costs. The full article is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Corporate and Municipal CUSIP request volumes dropped in January 2025; North American corporate requests at 4,505 down 36.9%, municipal requests decreased 14.1%.
news.summary: In the CUSIP Issuance Trends Report for January 2025 by CGS, it was noted that there was a decrease in new security identifier requests, signaling a slowdown in debt and capital markets activity. North American corporate CUSIP requests totaled 4,505, down by 36.9% monthly and 24.2% annually. U.S. corporate debt identifiers saw a 32.6% decline in request volume. Municipal requests fell 14.1% monthly but were up 1.8% year-over-year, with Texas leading in municipal requests. International equity CUSIP requests decreased by 19.5%, while international debt requests rose by 14.0%. Long-term municipal notes saw a significant increase of 362.5% year-to-date through January 2025 compared to the same period in 2024. Gerard Faulkner, CGS's Director of Operations, noted that although monthly request volumes seemed slow compared to late 2024, most asset classes showed gains compared to the previous year.

news.headline: BCC Research enhances its Custom Research and Consulting services to meet evolving business needs, offering tailored solutions aligning with unique organization goals. Expert guidance provided by global consultants and analysts helps clients achieve leadership in their industries and predicts market direction. Core services include custom market research and consulting services addressing various business challenges.
news.summary: BCC Research, a market research and consulting company based in Boston, offers Custom Research and Consulting services tailored to each client's needs. The company helps businesses enter new markets, develop innovative products, and gain insights into industry dynamics through a global team of consultants and analysts. Their services aim to help clients achieve leadership in their industries, launch new patents, and predict market trends. Core services include Custom Market Research, providing analysis of market trends and opportunities, and Consulting Services to address complex business challenges. BCC Research continues to innovate its offerings to meet the evolving needs of the business world. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Bitcoin and stocks drop as inflation rate spikes to 3.0%, fuel oil rises by 6.2%, Fed uncertain on rate cuts.
news.summary: The January inflation report has led to a change in market sentiment regarding Federal Reserve rate cuts. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) climbed by 3.0% year-over-year, surpassing expectations and marking the fourth consecutive increase. The monthly CPI surged by 0.5%, the largest leap since August 2023, with fuel oil rising by 6.2% and meat, poultry, fish, and egg prices also seeing significant increases. Market reactions have resulted in the first anticipated Fed rate cut being pushed to December 2025 and the second cut not expected until September 2026. This news has impacted investor expectations and the likelihood of a single rate cut in 2025 is now the most probable scenario.

Key companies such as Coinbase, Twilio, and Roku are dominating the earnings scene. Dow Jones Futures have seen a decline following the recent news on Trump tariffs, which pushed the market near its peak. Investors are advised to stay vigilant amidst this market activity.

news.headline: Telephone and Data Systems (TDS) revenue decline by 5.6% in Q4 to $1.240 billion, stock dips; UScellular revenue down 3% to $970 million.
news.summary: Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. (NYSE:TDS) saw a drop in its stock value following the release of its fourth-quarter FY24 earnings report. The company experienced a 5.6% decrease in fourth-quarter revenue compared to the previous year, reaching $1.240 billion, falling short of the analyst consensus of $1.254 billion. However, the adjusted EPS loss of 9 cents outperformed the expected loss of 28 cents.UScellular's revenue also declined by 3% to $970 million, yet Postpaid Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) showed a slight increase of 0.2% to $51.73. On the other hand, the TDS Telecom segment observed a positive trend with a 1% growth in revenues to $264 million, accompanied by a 2.4% rise in residential broadband connections.Overall, the company's earnings report and performance metrics reflect a mixed outcome, with some segments showing growth while others decline. For a comprehensive overview, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Quantum Key Distribution Market set to reach $2.63 billion by 2030, Toshiba and MagiQ Technologies leading. Rising data security concerns driving market growth. Advanced QKD solutions being developed. High implementation costs and lack of standardization are key restraints. Expansion opportunities in 5G, IoT networks, and satellite-based quantum communication. Several prominent companies including Toshiba, ID Quantique, and QuantumCTek at the forefront of market. Significant advancements in satellite-based QKD.
news.summary: The global Quantum Key Distribution Market is expected to grow from USD 0.48 billion in 2024 to USD 2.63 billion by 2030, with a CAGR of 32.6% during the forecast period. The increasing demand for Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) is driven by the need to enhance data security amidst rising cyber threats. Notably, a Proof of Concept (PoC) was successfully conducted to secure a high-capacity link with QKD between data centers in Athens. The market dynamics include rise in cyber attacks prompting investments in R&D, advancements in quantum communication infrastructure, and quantum computing. Key companies in the market include Toshiba, ID Quantique, QuintessenceLabs, and MagiQ Technologies among others. Opportunities lie in expanding into 5G, IoT networks, and satellite-based quantum communication while challenges include high implementation costs and lack of standardization. Trend-wise, advancements in satellite-based QKD are on the rise.

news.headline: January's inflation spikes to 3% YoY, with monthly rise since Aug 2023. Concerns over food, energy price hikes. Experts urge caution on impact of tariffs on future interest rates.
news.summary: Inflation for January exceeded expectations as the Consumer Price Index jumped to 3% year-over-year. This marks the highest monthly increase since August 2023, largely fueled by spikes in food and energy prices. Core inflation, excluding these volatile sectors, also saw a rise, suggesting broader economic concerns. Charlie Ripley, a senior investment strategist at Allianz Investment Management, warned of possible further price hikes due to President Donald Trump's tariffs not yet being factored in. This unexpected inflation surge has prompted speculation on future interest rate adjustments. Egg prices hitting a record high in January contributed to the inflation uptick. For more on this topic, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Wayfair Q4 results: Revenue beat at $3.12 billion, but net revenue flat y-o-y. Analysts cut PT to $46. Active customers at 21.4 million. Earnings miss due to high ad spend. EBITDA at $96.0 million.
news.summary: Wayfair Inc's (NYSE:W) shares dipped despite beating Q4 revenue expectations, with net revenue reaching $3.12 billion, flat year-on-year but surpassing the estimated $3.07 billion. The number of active customers totaled 21.4 million, falling short of the projected 22.2 million. Adjusted EBITDA stood at $96.0 million, missing the anticipated $105.1 million. Stifel analyst Mark Kelley reiterated a Hold rating, reducing the price target from $47 to $46 based on these figures. Analyst Bobby Griffin from Raymond James kept a Strong Buy rating and a price target of $60 despite the $10 million consensus miss in adjusted EBITDA. The earnings shortfall was attributed to increased ad spending compared to the previous quarter. Despite the setback, 1Q revenue guidance slightly exceeded expectations, with EBITDA margin projections aligning with forecasts at the midpoint.

news.headline: The computerized sewing and embroidery machine market is projected to grow at a 4.8% CAGR, reaching USD 3.6 billion by 2035 due to technological advancements and increased demand in fashion and textile industries. Industry leaders mentioned include Brother Industries, Janome Corporation, and more. Market reached USD 2.2 billion in 2024.
news.summary: The computerized sewing and embroidery machine market is thriving, with a value of US$2.2 billion in 2024. It is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 4.8% from 2025 to 2035, reaching US$3.6 billion by 2035. This growth is attributed to technological advancements, automation in textile production, and a rising demand for personalized solutions. These machines offer features like AI-driven error detection and IoT connectivity, transforming industrial and personal textile production. Key players driving this market include Brother Industries, Janome Corporation, and Bernina International AG. Significant developments include Bernina International's advanced Bernina 990 model and JANOME Corporation's adoption of Esol's Et-Kernel platform. The market snapshot reveals a promising future with a CAGR of 4.8% from 2025 to 2035.

news.headline: This week in cannabis: MedMen saga, multi-million-dollar financings, and controversies. Greenlane's $25M offering, Story Cannabis' $15M loan, and MN's lottery system causing delays. Simplifya partners with INCBA. Former MedMen CEO Adam Bierman's upcoming memoir reveals company's rise and fall.
news.summary: The cannabis industry has seen a week packed with political moves, market shifts, and controversies. In New Jersey, the Cannabis Market Spotlight event is set to take place on March 4. MedMen, a once-prominent company, faces more scrutiny with the release of former CEO Adam Bierman's memoir. On the policy front, Trump's pick for a top health lawyer opposes cannabis, and the DEA faces legal challenges over the rescheduling process. In business news, Greenlane Holdings' $25 million stock offering led to a 37% drop in shares, while Story Cannabis Co. secured a $15 million loan for expansion in Ohio. Minnesota's cannabis market faces delays, potential lottery system, and supply shortages. Simplifya partners with INCBA to enhance compliance solutions for attorneys. MedMen's rise and fall are detailed in Bierman's upcoming memoir, citing financial mismanagement as a key factor. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Fed Chair Powell: CPI above forecast at 3%, US debt path unsustainable. Moderating inflation but not enough for rate cuts.
news.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell affirmed the central bank's unwavering stance on monetary policy in a recent testimony to Congress. Powell emphasized that despite a slight moderation, inflation remains above desired levels, with the latest Consumer Price Index revealing an annual inflation rate of 3%, surpassing the anticipated 2.9%. Core inflation, excluding volatile food and energy prices, also exceeded expectations at 3.3%, indicating enduring pricing pressures. Powell acknowledged the progress made but emphasized the need for continued efforts to achieve the Fed's 2% inflation target. Despite the encouraging data, Powell cautioned against premature reactions, highlighting the necessity for patience in addressing the inflation outlook.

news.headline: THEON surpasses expectations with FY 2024 results and predicts continued growth in FY 2025.
Trump initially promised to reduce prices as President, but now acknowledges it will take more time to address inflation.
news.summary: During his campaign, President Trump promised to reduce costs from the first day in office. However, his administration now concedes that achieving this goal will require more time than initially anticipated.

news.headline: Global VSAT market to reach USD 22.0 billion by 2035 at 9.1% CAGR. VSAT valued at US$8.9 billion in 2024. Key players include Hughes, Viasat, Inmarsat, Gilat, and Cobham.
news.summary: The global Very Small Aperture Terminal (VSAT) market is valued at US$8.9 billion in 2024 and is expected to reach US$22.0 billion by 2035 with a projected CAGR of 9.1%. The growth is driven by the increasing demand for reliable satellite communications in remote areas, with industries such as telecommunications, defense, maritime, oil & gas, and enterprise networking adopting VSAT technology. Advancements in high-throughput satellites and LEO satellite networks are improving VSAT capabilities by reducing latency and lowering bandwidth costs. The market is forecasted to grow due to the rising demand for rural and remote connectivity, growth in maritime and aviation communications, advancements in LEO and MEO satellites, government and military investments, and the integration of 5G with satellite networks. Key players dominating the VSAT market include Hughes Network Systems, Viasat Inc., Inmarsat Global Ltd., Gilat Satellite Networks, and Cobham plc.

news.headline: Global Policy Advisors predicts Federal Reserve will play a significant role in U.S. sovereign wealth fund creation.
news.summary: Salar Ghahramani, a Global Policy Advisors analyst, predicts that the Federal Reserve will play a significant role in the creation of a U.S. sovereign wealth fund.

news.headline: S&P 500 nears all-time high, up 1.5% on the week, driven by strong corporate earnings and concerns over tariffs.

Key factors impacting the market include weaker-than-expected retail sales, potential tariffs, and geopolitical events. The S&P 500 has seen a 1.5% increase for the week and is close to hitting a new all-time high. Corporate earnings have been driving stock performance, with S&P 500 companies experiencing a 16.4% year-over-year earnings growth in the fourth quarter of 2021. Concerns over tariffs are significant, with 146 S&P 500 firms highlighting it as a worry during earnings calls — the highest number since mid-2019. For more information, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Stocks recover after inflation report; S&P down 0.2%, CPI rises 3% y/y, core inflation at 3.3% y/y, bond markets pressured.
news.summary: The news article discusses how a higher-than-expected inflation report initially caused market turmoil, with the S&P 500 down 0.2% and the Nasdaq 100 flat. Small-cap stocks fared worse, with the Russell 2000 slipping 0.8%. The Consumer Price Index rose by 3% year over year in January, surpassing economist forecasts and marking the fastest monthly inflation rate since August 2023. Core inflation, which excludes food and energy, also increased to 3.3% year over year. The inflation data led to a shift in expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts, with traders now projecting only one reduction by December.
